verdict: 2026-07-04 17:23:43 PDT — The storage direction is mostly right: for a capped 20-row aggregate history, piggybacking on the existing encrypted per-user blob is simpler than a new store and keeps export/delete coverage. Server-side shape validation and the 100kb body limit are a sound abuse bound. But the sketch is not merge-ready as written because it treats a history append like ordinary debounced settings state, and that creates a real persistence gap for the feature’s primary artifact.

findings.title: History append rides a debounced whole-config PUT
findings.claim: The sketch says `addMeetingSummary` updates config and rides the existing debounced `saveRates`. That path waits 800ms and then overwrites the entire encrypted config blob. A presenter who clicks End session and closes the panel quickly can lose the row, and a stale/older panel saving a config without `meetingHistory` can wipe existing history. That is a data-loss/backward-compat gap for the one thing this feature is meant to preserve.
findings.alternative: Keep the encrypted blob if desired, but make history append an explicit durable write: compute the next config and immediately flush it on End session, and have the server preserve/merge existing `meetingHistory` when the field is absent or stale. If concurrent-session correctness matters, use a small append endpoint backed by the same encrypted store.
findings.win: Preserves the reuse/export/delete benefits while eliminating quick-teardown loss and stale-client overwrites; if the server owns the merge, cap/order become centralized invariants.

findings.title: End snapshot needs an explicit dependency strategy
findings.claim: The current `sessionActions` object is memoized with an empty dependency array. The sketch adds reads of `totals`, `participants.length`, `config.costModel`, and `actions.addMeetingSummary` inside `end`. If implemented in place without changing that shape, the saved total/duration refs can be current while headcount, cost model, and $/min come from the initial render.
findings.alternative: Either include the dynamic summary inputs/actions in the `useMemo` dependency list, or keep `end` stable by maintaining a dedicated `summaryInputsRef` updated each render, parallel to `liveRef`.
findings.win: Avoids silently wrong historical rows and gives the store-wiring test a concrete invariant: End session records the current model, current headcount, and current cost rate.

findings.title: Stored history needs user-facing privacy disclosure
findings.claim: Export/delete coverage is inherited, and aggregate-only rows are much safer than names/rates. But `meetingHistory` still adds server-stored meeting metadata: ended time, duration, total cost, headcount, and model. Existing copy describes the persisted data mainly as the private value table, so the sketch would understate what leaves the browser.
findings.alternative: Update the in-app saved-data notice and README/privacy copy to say aggregate past-meeting summaries are saved with the presenter config, decryptable by the operator, and included in export/delete; keep the no-names/no-per-person-rates boundary explicit.
findings.win: Maintains the privacy promise without adding a new privacy store or endpoint, and makes the inherited export/delete behavior understandable to users.
